headline: Rail Network Density Lowest Among ASEAN-5 Countries
context: Malaysia has only 2.1km of rail per 100,000 population the lowest among ASEAN-5 behind Thailand at 3.8km.

cards.big: Malaysia rail density of 2.1km per 100,000 is lowest among ASEAN-5
cards.sub: Decades of highway prioritization left rail infrastructure severely underdeveloped

cards.big: Total operational rail in Malaysia is 2,800km versus 12,400km in Thailand
cards.sub: Malaysia land area is 75% of Thailand but rail network only 23% as extensive

cards.big: Rail carries only 3.8% of passenger trips versus 18% in Thailand
cards.sub: Bus and private vehicle dependency results from insufficient rail connectivity

cards.big: RMK-13 allocated 28% of transport budget to rail up from 15% prior
cards.sub: Shift is significant but experts say 45% needed to close gap by 2040

cards.big: Rail density tests whether Malaysia can reverse decades of highway-first policy
Lowest rail density among ASEAN-5 — Malaysia built highways where it needed railways over 40 years.
